seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: generalize the input from a plain conflict graph to a weighted graph with per-edge required channel separation d, and change the objective from counting same-channel conflicts to minimizing a separation-graded interference penalty w*max(0,d-|c_u-c_v|); this turns bounded-K frequency assignment into a weighted T-coloring / minimum-interference FAP that is NP-hard and admits diverging greedy / local-search / restart heuristics.
title: Reef Hydrophone Channel Assignment: Silencing Survey Interference
strategies: trivial baseline: put every hydrophone on channel 1 (pays the full same-channel penalty on every pair)
one-pass greedy: process hydrophones in index order, assigning each the channel that minimizes added interference with already-assigned neighbors
local search: greedy init then repeatedly move each hydrophone to its best channel given all current neighbors until a fixed point
randomized multi-restart local search: run local search from several seeded random/greedy inits and keep the assignment with the lowest total interference
